data.title: UAE holds over 400 MW power capacity in GCC data center market dominated by Saudi Arabia, hosting 110+ data centers.
data.description: Dublin - A new database titled "GCC Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's collection. The upcoming data center market in the GCC is heavily influenced by Saudi Arabia, holding nearly 60% of the total power capacity. The GCC region currently accommodates over 110 operational data centers, with notable presence in countries like the UAE and Saudi Arabia. The United Arab Emirates leads the existing market with a power capacity exceeding 400 MW, thanks to key industry players in the region.

data.title: Tickets available for Life Fest event on Jan. 23 in Oxon Hill, Maryland, organized by Knights of Columbus and Sisters of Life for pre-March for Life rally; thousands expected to attend.
data.description: A significant event is scheduled to take place on January 23, 2026, in Oxon Hill, Maryland. The Knights of Columbus and Sisters of Life are joining forces once more to organize a pre-March for Life rally called Life Fest. This event is expected to bring together thousands of individuals for a powerful gathering. The rally will feature live performances and various activities as part of the event lineup.

data.title: Holmes Murphy appoints Lindsay Chase to its Board of Managers, emphasizing independence as a privately held company.
data.description: Holmes Murphy, a prominent company based in DES MOINES, Iowa, recently reinforced its privately held status with the launch of its updated brand campaign titled "That's the Power of Independence." This initiative comes into effect as of Jan. 13, 2026. As one of the largest privately held companies in the nation, Holmes Murphy is making significant strides with two major announcements. This move reaffirms the company's commitment to operating independently and highlights its dedication to serving its clients' needs with autonomy and excellence.

data.title: CUPE warns of threats to public services in Newfoundland & Labrador in 2026 due to funding cuts, P3s, AI use.
data.description: In St. John’s, Newfoundland & Labrador, the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) is warning that 2026 will pose significant challenges for defending public services in the province. CUPE anticipates an uphill battle in protecting services without strong public pushback against the provincial government. Members across various public sectors are facing assaults on services due to funding slashes, service cutbacks, adoption of public-private partnerships (P3s), and heightened reliance on artificial intelligence. The government is being accused of systematically dismantling public services, indicating a concerning trend.

data.title: ExxonMobil (NYSE: XOM) to announce Q4 2025 financial results on Jan 30, 2026, at 5:30 a.m. CT via Business Wire.
data.description: Exxon Mobil Corporation (NYSE: XOM) is set to publish its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on Friday, January 30, 2026. The release will be made available at 5:30 a.m. CT on investor.exxonmobil.com through a press release distributed by Business Wire. The key executives to be noted during the announcement are Darren Woods, the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer; Kathy Mikells, the Senior Vice President and current Chief Financial Officer; along with Neil Hansen, who will be assuming the role of Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer from February 1, 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin rises to $93,000 following positive CPI report, encouraging investors with $189.5 million liquidated in 24 hours.
data.description: Bitcoin has surpassed the $93,000 mark following a positive CPI report relieving concerns about rate hikes, leading to a rise in risk assets sentiment. The current prices of top cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $93,509.76, Ethereum at $3,198.80, Solana at $143.40, XRP at $2.10, Dogecoin at $0.1433, and Shiba Inu at $0.058779. Recent data reveals that 88,679 traders were liquidated in the last day, totaling $189.50 million in losses. Additionally, there have been net inflows of $116.7 million in spot Bitcoin ETFs and $5.04 million in spot Ethereum ETFs. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: ResourceTec Corp appoints Dr. Deepak Malholtra and Mr. Barry Miller to its Board of Directors on Jan. 8, 2026.
data.description: West Jordan, Utah - On January 13, 2026, ResourceTec Corporation (OTCID: RREE) announced the appointment of Dr. Deepak Malholtra and Mr. Barry Miller to the Company's Board of Directors on January 08, 2026. Mr. Miller also assumed another role within the Company - undisclosed number of members were added to the board, enhancing its leadership with two new accomplished individuals.

data.title: America's Test Kitchen offers advertising access across its FAST Channel, reaching a food-focused audience - PRNewswire 01/13/26.
data.description: ATK has unveiled a new opportunity for advertisers to connect with a highly engaged, food-oriented audience through brand-safe FAST Channel inventory. The renowned culinary media brand, America's Test Kitchen (ATK), revealed this initiative on January 13, 2026. Widely recognized and trusted by millions, ATK is providing a platform for advertisers to reach a receptive audience.
